00-000-24169660.sentence: The framework centers on the concept that automation enables capital to replace labor in tasks it was previously engaged in [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800].

00-001-98dd3ec7.sentence: This replacement shifts the task content of production against labor because of a displacement effect [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800].

00-002-9db1c0f6.sentence: However, the effects of automation are counterbalanced by the creation of new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800].

00-003-86ad8c0c.sentence: This reinstatement effect can offset the displacement of workers, but the balance between these two forces determines the net impact on employment [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:100-900].

00-004-1cc4ce3a.sentence: In their empirical decomposition, the authors find that the slower growth of employment over the last three decades is accounted for by an acceleration in the displacement effect [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:400-1200].

00-005-03f2c8ce.sentence: This acceleration is especially pronounced in manufacturing [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:500-1300].

00-006-e9d2a8ba.sentence: In contrast, the reinstatement effect has become weaker over the same period [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:500-1300].

00-007-b549a281.sentence: Moreover, productivity growth has been slower than in previous decades [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:531-1331].

00-008-dd3d46e9.sentence: Thus, while automation displaces workers from existing tasks, the failure of new task creation to keep pace has led to a net reduction in employment growth [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:400-1200].

00-009-84f45fe6.sentence: The framework highlights the importance of both displacement and reinstatement in shaping labor market outcomes [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:100-900].

00-010-8cb340e1.sentence: Although automation can increase productivity, its labor-displacing effects may dominate if new tasks are not created [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800].

00-011-f6427c35.sentence: The acceleration of displacement, particularly in manufacturing, has been a key driver of this trend [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:500-1300].

00-012-a8885b69.sentence: Overall, the framework provides a lens to understand how technological change can have divergent effects on labor markets depending on the relative strength of displacement and reinstatement [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800].

00-013-938e6beb.sentence: They often ignore the strong complementarities between automation and labor that increase productivity, raise earnings, and augment demand for labor [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5200-6000].

00-014-2f0b306f.sentence: In contrast, the paper emphasizes that tasks that cannot be substituted by automation are generally complemented by it [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:8300-9100].

00-015-3b0a37a3.sentence: However, the prevailing discourse frequently overlooks these complementarities [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5100-5900].

00-016-a039d3ea.sentence: Although many fear widespread job displacement, the paper argues that such fears are exaggerated because complementarities offset substitution effects [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5100-5900].

00-017-c8b35c01.sentence: The complementarity argument is central: tasks that are not automated are made more valuable by automation [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:6800-7600].

00-018-682614ab.sentence: Despite the common assumption that machines simply replace workers, the paper demonstrates that automation and labor often work together [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:6400-7200].

00-019-d5920c54.sentence: The strong complementarities are a key reason why employment has not collapsed in the face of advancing technology [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5200-6000].

00-020-f980f135.sentence: Thus, the paper’s thesis corrects the overstatement of substitution and highlights the ignored complementarities [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5100-5900].

01-000-c2933635.sentence: Identification strategy: variation in exposure to robots—defined from industry-level advances in robotics and local industry employment [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-003-7dec4aac.sentence: Method: using a Gaussian process classifier [#ev:frey_osborne_computerisation:1000-1800].

01-005-1be02998.sentence: Their identification strategy exploited variation in exposure to robots across local labor markets [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-006-503aefc5.sentence: This exposure was constructed from two distinct data sources: industry-level advances in robotics and local industry employment [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-007-5c198401.sentence: By interacting these two components, the researchers generated a measure of robot exposure that varied across labor markets and over time [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-008-61991b12.sentence: Labor markets with a high concentration of employment in industries that were rapidly adopting robots received a high exposure value [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-009-ee004cc7.sentence: This differential exposure provided the identifying variation necessary to estimate the effects of robots [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-010-0cb92f84.sentence: The variation in robot exposure was not uniform across US labor markets [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-011-1d503d28.sentence: Some labor markets were more exposed than others due to their industrial composition [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-012-b1d765e5.sentence: The exposure measure thus captured the potential impact of robots on a given labor market [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-013-5f130785.sentence: The study focused on US labor markets as the population of interest [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-014-d780ecfe.sentence: The primary outcomes examined were employment and wages [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-015-fa6cc7a0.sentence: The analysis related changes in these outcomes to the differential robot exposure across labor markets [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_robots_jobs:0-688].

01-016-e4ddfd8d.sentence: The groundbreaking study by Frey and Osborne assessed how susceptible various occupations are to becoming computerised [#ev:frey_osborne_computerisation:1100-1900].

01-017-9808e289.sentence: The resulting exposure measure was the probability of computerisation [#ev:frey_osborne_computerisation:1000-1800].

01-018-d0ddf756.sentence: The Gaussian process classifier was central to generating these nuanced probabilities [#ev:frey_osborne_computerisation:1000-1800].

02-003-0a1b8aa3.sentence: The study employed a staggered introduction design [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-004-217a9b11.sentence: The population consisted of 5,172 customer-support agents [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-005-56251c13.sentence: The intervention was a generative AI–based conversational assistant [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-006-40d0ad6c.sentence: This assistant utilized generative artificial intelligence technology [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-007-b3137eb7.sentence: The staggered introduction design allowed for the intervention to be deployed at different times [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-009-8a1b097d.sentence: These agents were engaged in customer-support roles [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-010-c3ec51bf.sentence: The population of 5,172 customer-support agents was the study sample [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-011-dc3451cc.sentence: The intervention was a conversational assistant powered by generative AI [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-012-63551879.sentence: The staggered introduction design involves non-simultaneous intervention rollout [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-013-35207c67.sentence: The 5,172 customer-support agents were the participants [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-014-d2a16248.sentence: The generative AI–based conversational assistant was the intervention under evaluation [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-015-0936a723.sentence: The staggered introduction design was used to roll out the intervention [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-017-5bfe13d2.sentence: The intervention was based on generative AI [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-018-40f18ee7.sentence: The assistant was a conversational agent [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-019-8675110c.sentence: The design was a staggered introduction [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

02-021-56251c13.sentence: The intervention was a generative AI–based conversational assistant [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

03-000-028c9bc4.sentence: Johnson to empanel a National Commission on Technology, Automation, and Economic Progress in 1964 [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:1300-2100].

03-001-71bb0025.sentence: That commission concluded that technology eliminates jobs, not work, yet recommended measures including a guaranteed minimum income and government as employer of last resort [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:2300-3100].

03-002-8a7d222f.sentence: Overall, the literature underscores that while AI-driven automation poses displacement risks, its net effect on employment depends on the balance between substitution and complementarity, the creation of new tasks, and the responsiveness of skill supply [#ev:autor_why_still_jobs:5100-5900][#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:0-800][#ev:ev_025:200-1000].

04-000-bef48cbb.sentence: One systematic review of the AI-employment literature concludes that the impact is multifaceted, creating new high-skilled positions and interdisciplinary demand while simultaneously displacing low-skilled routine roles and polarizing employment structures [#ev:ev_025:600-1400].

04-001-ab1acd49.sentence: In the United States between 2010 and 2018, a one‑standard‑deviation increase in AI‑related education and skill investments was associated with an 18.1% rise in employment, alongside 19.5% higher sales and 22.3% greater market valuation [#ev:ev_329:1700-2500].

04-002-cde9ffe3.sentence: Job applicants possessing AI capital in the UK in 2022 enjoyed a 14.4% wage premium and a 22.3‑percentage‑point higher chance of receiving interview invitations compared to those without such capital [#ev:ev_329:3000-3800].

04-003-5362887a.sentence: A systematic review covering 2020–2025 finds that AI adoption is closely associated with rising demand for technical and interdisciplinary skills and with widening wage gaps between AI‑skilled and non‑AI‑skilled workers [#ev:ev_338:0-800].

04-004-89248120.sentence: The IZA review further highlights that AI can help industries stabilize employment during economic downturns and enhance job quality for groups historically reliant on physical strength [#ev:ev_329:400-1200].

04-005-9fcf4d8f.sentence: Evidence from a multi‑study synthesis suggests that many jobs are not eliminated but transformed, as AI frequently operates in augmentation rather than pure substitution mode [#ev:ev_338:0-800].

04-006-c8201f76.sentence: A survey of 138 educators in Bihor, Romania, underlines the imperative for educational systems to shape AI‑related competencies, as both the education and business sectors significantly influence career preparedness for an AI‑driven labor market [#ev:ev_396:600-1400].

05-000-17ec7f2c.sentence: This fear negatively affects job satisfaction, particularly for low-skilled workers, consistent with models of skill-biased displacement [#ev:ev_266:1000-1800].

05-001-e230acbf.sentence: However, measuring AI adoption remains challenging: patent-based metrics overstate advanced economies and large firms, while job posting analyses and task mapping approaches are not yet standardized [#ev:ev_333:5100-5900].

05-002-74d3d645.sentence: A review of the economics of AI notes a stylized fact that AI impacts employment differently across skill levels, exacerbating income inequality both within and between countries [#ev:ev_169:700-1500].

05-003-2f9da539.sentence: Overall, the interplay of displacement, reinstatement, skill-biased demand shifts, and augmentation defines the mechanisms through which AI restructures labor markets, with measurement of exposure remaining an active area of development [#ev:acemoglu_restrepo_automation_tasks:100-900][#ev:ev_333:500-1300][#ev:ev_338:500-1300].

06-000-2f75cbc4.sentence: AI-driven labor market restructuring is reshaping employment structures across economies, with evidence from multiple countries indicating that AI increases demand for high-skilled workers while displacing low-skilled, routine-intensive roles [#ev:ev_004:5400-6200][#ev:ev_025:600-1400][#ev:ev_329:900-1700].

06-001-21a4bcb6.sentence: For instance, a study of 5,172 customer-support agents found that access to a generative AI-based assistant increased productivity by 15% on average, with less-experienced and lower-skilled workers improving both speed and quality, while the most experienced saw small gains in speed and small declines in quality [#ev:brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800].

06-002-74b80655.sentence: Educational systems and business sectors must collaborate to shape AI-related competencies, as a PLS-SEM analysis of 138 educators in Romania found significant influences from both sectors on career preparedness [#ev:ev_396:500-1300].

06-003-6b161edf.sentence: At the firm level, AI adoption is concentrated in large enterprises; in the US in 2017, only 5.8% of companies used AI, but when weighted by employment, the adoption rate rose to 18.2%, indicating that large firms are driving AI integration [#ev:ev_338:10700-11500].

06-004-30a11fcf.sentence: AI adoption also affects worker well-being: in Norway, automation induced 40% of employed workers to fear that their work might be replaced by a smart machine, and this fear negatively affected job satisfaction, particularly among low-skilled workers [#ev:ev_266:800-1600].

06-005-7f068e3d.sentence: Similarly, in Australia, AI-exposed individuals experienced lower satisfaction with job security and overall job satisfaction, although they reported increased perceived autonomy [#ev:ev_328:800-1600].

06-006-0c9a1e18.sentence: Wage inequality is a growing concern, as AI adoption widens wage gaps between AI-skilled and non-AI-skilled workers [#ev:ev_338:0-800], and in the UK, job applicants with AI capital had approximately 14.4% higher wage prospects and a 22.3 percentage point higher chance of receiving interview invitations [#ev:ev_329:2900-3700].

06-007-9dee9df9.sentence: Ethical considerations are paramount, with reviews identifying risks such as algorithmic bias, disinformation, and the need for robust regulation to prevent misuse and ensure transparency [#ev:ev_180:700-1500][#ev:ev_384:4500-5300].

06-008-6caabb29.sentence: Policymakers are urged to promote inclusive AI development policies to prevent widening digital divides and unemployment, and to ensure equitable opportunities [#ev:ev_329:0-800][#ev:ev_026:900-1700].

06-009-563920ab.sentence: Moreover, investments in digital infrastructure, such as high-speed internet, can boost employment opportunities by 13.2% and total employment per company by up to 22% [#ev:ev_026:1100-1900].

06-010-191a9b0f.sentence: Ultimately, the future of work in the AI era will be determined less by technology itself than by how organizations, institutions, and policies govern its implementation [#ev:ev_338:200-1000].

06-011-2b9de8eb.sentence: However, many jobs are not eliminated but transformed, as AI frequently functions in augmentation rather than pure substitution mode [#ev:ev_338:500-1300].

07-000-0090e757.sentence: A fundamental limitation of the existing evidence base is the lack of standardized, validated metrics for AI exposure [#ev:ev_333:5100-5900].

07-001-b4d1ee0d.sentence: This measurement heterogeneity is compounded by geographic concentration: a large share of empirical work draws on data from single countries, particularly China [#ev:ev_004:0-800], Norway [#ev:ev_266:4400-5200], and the United States [#ev:ev_025:3500-4300][#ev:frey_osborne_computerisation:1000-1800], while evidence for Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and informal economies remains scarce [#ev:ev_333:6900-7700].

07-002-425d4c5a.sentence: Additional gaps concern the psychosocial and ethical dimensions of AI's labor market effects [#ev:ev_333:6800-7600][#ev:ev_424:0-800].

07-003-a5e32269.sentence: The psychosocial consequences of algorithmic management, surveillance, and deskilling receive far less attention than quantitative employment outcomes [#ev:ev_333:6300-7100].

07-004-9be4a5b2.sentence: Consensus on measures of AI adoption are lacking, making cross-country and cross-sectoral analysis difficult [#ev:ev_333:5200-6000].

07-005-8f6c5eaf.sentence: Studies have used robot adoption and industrial robots as instruments to study the effects of AI or automation [#ev:ev_004:0-800][#ev:ev_266:300-1100]

07-006-29244b89.sentence: High-skilled workers, who report stronger fear of replacement, tend to follow media debates on automation [#ev:ev_266:2200-3000].
